sentences.text: Hey, good morning, Corey, how are you?
I know about this boat rewards update.
Yeah, so, I guess we turned that around super quick, so hopefully they should be psyched about that.
It's just… I don't think it's anything crazy.
My understanding is that it's just kind of a tag.
There was more to it than that.
Yeah, I guess we've… we're not able to… Show how it converts… $2.
That sounded like the thing they wanted.
So, this is kind of the first step.
I'll just frame it like this is our first step, kind of our first pass, and we're gonna keep iterating on it.
Yeah, I think that makes sense.
I don't want to oversell it based on that.
Yeah, it was kind of gonna be, like, a little after, like, hey, by the way, also, this is happening, this is our first swing.
I've got the three of them in the waiting room.
I don't have Heath yet, but I figure we're pretty light on the agenda.
Remind me who the third person is.
It sounds like she's kind of stepping in to help out with Hamza, and maybe Michelle will start to step away.
Yeah, she's been on a few of these calls now.
She been at… was she at the in-person event?
I don't think we met her there, but we're definitely… she's been on the last few of these.
I'll keep an eye out for if Heath is joining.
Little bit… still feeling like, like the Labor Day weekend could have been longer.
I still feel that way, Hansa.
Who do we talk to about this?
Is there a suggestion box that you guys have, or… I don't know.
Did anybody do any traveling, or did everybody stay local?
Remind me, Jennifer, where's local for you?
I keep hearing great things about Charlotte.
Is there any kind of spooky aspect to Charlotte?
I know it's, like, a really old city, older town.
I don't do a lot of spooky, so I'm not the expert.
Neither do you… I don't love doing it at night.
I've gotta do the spooky tour during the day.
Yeah, I just know that we have, like, some really fantastic, like, haunted houses, like, in the Raleigh area, but not… maybe in Charlotte?
Maybe in Charlotte, but, like, you have to sign a waiver, it's nonsense.
We have a director here who, with his wife, every day in October, watches a horror movie, every night.
So, like, we… we have nothing in common.
I'd, like, I enjoy working with him, but nothing in common, so…
Yeah, everyone, it's been a little bit.
Fine… sitting at a desk, not traveling today, for.
Really, really lighter agenda on our end for today.
wanted to just confirm that we've got the property website transitions complete.
Hamza, I know you're super plugged into that, and Brooke let us know, I believe it was last week, that we're all set there.
billing, they did confirm they received the quarterly payment.
I apologize for the confusion that happened.
I know you guys hit the stop order on the monthly.
Corey and I were… I think that's gonna be fine, Corey, that we do the quarterly payment, so…
Brief moment of panic, and then we're in a good spot.
Me too, because I was afraid they'd be like, no, we don't do this.
She's like, it's easier on us.
Not that you used all caps, Michelle, but in my mind, you sent an all-caps email to me, I sent an all-caps SLAC to several people here, like, why did you do this without warning, so…
It's okay, it's okay, it all worked out fine.
And then, planning for the 2026 onboarding.
I know I missed last call, but one of George's notes was that it sounds like we're gonna be having another tranche of properties to come in towards, the start of the new year.
So, Michelle, are you driving that?
Hamza, Jennifer, happy to set some time, maybe outside of this call, perhaps next week, to really plan for that.

Yeah, Jennifer's gonna be taking the lead, but I'll probably assist, Just to get it going, and we're probably going to onboard the rest of the properties who we've missed, in that initial December run we did some time ago.
what's your timeline or the expectation where you'd like everybody in?
Before the end of the year, as long as they're in by 1-1, because I think billing is also going to change the start of next year, so ideally, we want everyone in by then.
Yeah, that should be no problem.
I think we've got the process pretty well ironed out now.
So, Hamza, Jennifer, I'll shoot you guys some availabilities for next week, and we can iron that out.
Hamza, are you talking about… Hamza, are you talking about, like, the Pack Urban stuff that we're getting, or are you talking about a different group of properties?
Just onboarding people who aren't already signed up with… Oh, okay.
I see what you're talking about.
I thought you were talking about the… the other buses.
The Pacific Urban properties, that's good?
We haven't taken them on yet, we're about to.
Do they… Corey, do you work with Pac Urban?
Do they… I think they use ApartmentIQ already.
Okay, so the good news is, Phil, so we're taking over about 12,000 units in about 2 months.
Since they already use ApartmentIQ, I don't think we need the training piece of it, but we already need to figure out, Jennifer and Hamza, like.
We want to make sure they're on our instance, so that we all have the right access, and that we're billing correctly.
Corey, we'll figure that part out.
Yeah, I think we have… I think we have all of their units on, so, awesome.
I know they're operators, can I ask who you're… Taken over for?
Cushman… I think… and it's public now, because Cushman has been told.
We're taking over from Cushman and Wakefield.
But only about half of them, I think some of the East Coast stuff is going to Avenue 5?
Avenue 5, is that what they're called?
Yeah, we'll work through it, we'll make sure they're over in your instance, and then, happy to talk through, kind of, the rate that they're at, and how we want to deal with this, so…
And then, small update, you guys talked to us about the built rewards.
We've kind of taken our first swing at getting that into the tool.
It sounded like the big thing was, hey, how are we gonna maybe reflect that if they apply it to rent, is that right?
how, built rewards get factored into NER costs, or did I miss… characterize that.

I… I don't know… well, and for me, and maybe someone else had brought it up, but for me, it was less of… of getting it built into NER, but it was more of, like, making sure that that was a listed concession.
Or that… or maybe not… maybe not listed concession, but that they are part of Built Alliance, because…
So maybe that just becomes, are they part of it or not?
With all of these major companies starting to use it soon, I think that almost might need to be aligned for y'all, because there's going to be a lot of people.
So we've taken our first swing at it, and we're definitely there where we can now say, hey, they are participating.
You'll find it as an amenity, a new community amenity, whether they take.
And I'm thinking that the next iteration of this is kind of how is it being reflected?
Is it going to rent, or is it kind of another kind of concession?
So, just want to let you know, we heard the feedback.
Thank you for that, because I don't think we had been considering that too highly before, but it's in there, and we're going to keep iterating on it.
Yeah, I love seeing these things kind of turn around really quick, so… Always fun.
Anything on your end, guys, today?
I have the report ready, that… how we want our standard Willowbridge report to look.
So, I will send that over to you, and then, I think we're… next steps were to figure out if there's a way we can get, like.
have… I don't know if it defaults to that, or if we can just have it listed as… for all of our properties all the time, to, you know, pull it the Willowbridge way.
I don't know what you're… we kind of talked about some different ideas, I don't know where.
I think it'd be really cool if we bring maybe Mitch on our next call, and let's see what he thinks might be the best way to roll forward, and kind of develop this in concert.
He can tell you exactly what's possible there, and we'll find the best solution.
I think, I think there's a lot possible as far as the default settings.
We have created those default reports in the past.

So I think right now it's just juggling how ApartmentIQ is configured today, and how we want to make sure that your reports always look the way you want them, and take advantage of new things that come in.
Because we know things can be pretty static.

If we get a new metric, we have to rerun the report and add that metric, so now that it's so customizable, it's just added another level of complexity, that I think Mitch will help us kind of iron out and make sure we get Everything you guys need.
Excellent, so I'll have him on the next call.
I'll have them take a look at that stuff when you send it over, Michelle.
And then Jennifer Hamza, I'll reach out to you with some availability for next week, and we can start planning for the… the stragglers.
Heath, Corey, anything to add before we split?
Nothing for me, just wanted to say hi.
Yeah, same here for my son.
